description: IntegrationSink sends events to generic event sink

spec.description: Spec defines the desired state of the IntegrationSink.

arn.description: The S3 Bucket name or Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
arn.title: Bucket Name

autoCreateBucket.description: Specifies to automatically create the S3 bucket.
autoCreateBucket.title: Autocreate Bucket

delay.description: The number of milliseconds before the next poll of the selected bucket.
delay.title: Delay

deleteAfterRead.description: Specifies to delete objects after consuming them.
deleteAfterRead.title: Auto-delete Objects

destinationBucket.description: Define the destination bucket where an object must be moved when moveAfterRead is set to true.
destinationBucket.title: Destination Bucket

destinationBucketPrefix.description: Define the destination bucket prefix to use when an object must be moved, and moveAfterRead is set to true.
destinationBucketPrefix.title: Destination Bucket Prefix

destinationBucketSuffix.description: Define the destination bucket suffix to use when an object must be moved, and moveAfterRead is set to true.
destinationBucketSuffix.title: Destination Bucket Suffix

forcePathStyle.description: Forces path style when accessing AWS S3 buckets.
forcePathStyle.title: Force Path Style

ignoreBody.description: If true, the S3 Object body is ignored. Setting this to true overrides any behavior defined by the `includeBody` option. If false, the S3 object is put in the body.
ignoreBody.title: Ignore Body

maxMessagesPerPoll.description: Gets the maximum number of messages as a limit to poll at each polling. Gets the maximum number of messages as a limit to poll at each polling. The default value is 10. Use 0 or a negative number to set it as unlimited.
maxMessagesPerPoll.title: Max Messages Per Poll

moveAfterRead.description: Move objects from S3 bucket to a different bucket after they have been retrieved.
moveAfterRead.title: Move Objects After Delete

overrideEndpoint.description: Select this option to override the endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also provide a URI for the `uriEndpointOverride` option.
overrideEndpoint.title: Endpoint Overwrite

prefix.description: The AWS S3 bucket prefix to consider while searching.
prefix.title: Prefix

uriEndpointOverride.description: The overriding endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also select the `overrideEndpoint` option.
uriEndpointOverride.title: Overwrite Endpoint URI

arn.description: The SNS topic name name or Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
arn.title: Topic Name

autoCreateTopic.description: Setting the autocreation of the SNS topic.
autoCreateTopic.title: Autocreate Topic

overrideEndpoint.description: Select this option to override the endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also provide a URI for the `uriEndpointOverride` option.
overrideEndpoint.title: Endpoint Overwrite

uriEndpointOverride.description: The overriding endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also select the `overrideEndpoint` option.
uriEndpointOverride.title: Overwrite Endpoint URI

arn.description: The SQS Queue Name or ARN
arn.title: Queue Name

autoCreateQueue.description: Setting the autocreation of the SQS queue.
autoCreateQueue.title: Autocreate Queue

delay.description: The number of milliseconds before the next poll of the selected stream
delay.title: Delay

greedy.description: If greedy is enabled, then the polling will happen immediately again, if the previous run polled 1 or more messages.
greedy.title: Greedy Scheduler

host.description: The hostname of the Amazon AWS cloud.
host.title: AWS Host

maxMessagesPerPoll.description: The maximum number of messages to return. Amazon SQS never returns more messages than this value (however, fewer messages might be returned). Valid values 1 to 10. Default 1.
maxMessagesPerPoll.title: Max Messages Per Poll

overrideEndpoint.description: Select this option to override the endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also provide a URI for the `uriEndpointOverride` option.
overrideEndpoint.title: Endpoint Overwrite

protocol.description: The underlying protocol used to communicate with SQS
protocol.title: Protocol

queueURL.description: The full SQS Queue URL (required if using KEDA)
queueURL.title: Queue URL

uriEndpointOverride.description: The overriding endpoint URI. To use this option, you must also select the `overrideEndpoint` option.
uriEndpointOverride.title: Overwrite Endpoint URI

visibilityTimeout.description: The duration (in seconds) that the received messages are hidden from subsequent retrieve requests after being retrieved by a ReceiveMessage request.
visibilityTimeout.title: Visibility Timeout

waitTimeSeconds.description: The duration (in seconds) for which the call waits for a message to arrive in the queue before returning. If a message is available, the call returns sooner than WaitTimeSeconds. If no messages are available and the wait time expires, the call does not return a message list.
waitTimeSeconds.title: Wait Time Seconds

logMask.description: Mask sensitive information like password or passphrase in the log
logMask.title: Log Mask

loggerName.description: Name of the logging category to use
loggerName.title: Logger Name

marker.description: An optional Marker name to use
marker.title: Marker

multiline.description: If enabled then each information is outputted on a newline
multiline.title: Multiline

showAllProperties.description: Show all of the exchange properties (both internal and custom)
showAllProperties.title: Show All Properties

showCachedStreams.description: Whether Camel should show cached stream bodies or not.
showCachedStreams.title: Show Cached Streams

showExchangePattern.description: Shows the Message Exchange Pattern (or MEP for short)
showExchangePattern.title: Show Exchange Pattern

showProperties.description: Show the exchange properties (only custom). Use showAllProperties to show both internal and custom properties.
showProperties.title: Show Properties

showStreams.description: Show the stream bodies (they may not be available in following steps)
showStreams.title: Show Streams

status.description: Status represents the current state of the IntegrationSink. This data may be out of date.

address.description: IntegrationSink is Addressable. It exposes the endpoints as URIs to get events delivered into the used Kamelet.

addresses.description: IntegrationSink is Addressable. It exposes the endpoints as URIs to get events delivered into the used Kamelet.

annotations.description: Annotations is additional Status fields for the Resource to save some additional State as well as convey more information to the user. This is roughly akin to Annotations on any k8s resource, just the reconciler conveying richer information outwards.

conditions.description: Conditions the latest available observations of a resource's current state.

lastTransitionTime.description: LastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another. We use VolatileTime in place of metav1.Time to exclude this from creating equality.Semantic differences (all other things held constant).

message.description: A human readable message indicating details about the transition.

reason.description: The reason for the condition's last transition.

severity.description: Severity with which to treat failures of this type of condition. When this is not specified, it defaults to Error.

status.description: Status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

observedGeneration.description: ObservedGeneration is the 'Generation' of the Service that was last processed by the controller.

apiVersion.description: The API version of the applied EventPolicy. This indicates, which version of EventPolicy is supported by the resource.

name.description: The name of the applied EventPolicy
